speaker: patient
text: Hi, Doctor. I’ve been feeling a bit tired lately and noticed some weight gain over the past few months.

speaker: doctor
text: Thanks for coming in. Let’s start by reviewing your current medications—I see you’re on Atorvastatin 40mg every night, prescribed back in October.

speaker: doctor
text: You were also diagnosed with hypothyroidism last October, and we’ve been managing that with levothyroxine.

speaker: doctor
text: Your most recent lab work from October showed a hemoglobin level of 110 g/L—slightly below the normal range for adult women.

speaker: doctor
text: Your fasting blood glucose was 6.6 mmol/L at that time, which is in the prediabetic range.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, I’ve been trying to watch my diet, but I’m still not sure if my energy levels are related to the thyroid or something else.

speaker: doctor
text: That’s a good question. Fatigue can stem from several factors—hypothyroidism, anemia, or even blood sugar fluctuations. How’s your appetite and bowel habits been? Any cold intolerance or dry skin?

speaker: patient
text: Definitely more cold-sensitive, and my skin has been drier. I’ve also had some constipation lately.

speaker: doctor
text: Those are classic signs—we’ll check your TSH and free T4 today to see if your levothyroxine dose needs adjustment. Also, let’s repeat the CBC to reassess your hemoglobin.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure reading today was 96 mmHg—elevated, especially alongside the other findings.

speaker: doctor
text: Wait—let me recheck that. Actually, this second reading shows 80 mmHg, so it looks like the first was likely affected by white-coat anxiety.

speaker: patient
text: Oh, good—I’ve been monitoring at home, and it’s usually around 78–82 mmHg diastolic.

speaker: doctor
text: That’s reassuring. Let’s also look at your glucose control. Did you check your blood sugar recently?

speaker: doctor
text: Your home glucose reading this morning was 4.9 mmol/L—much better than the 6.6 we saw in October. That suggests improved dietary adherence or possible early response to lifestyle changes.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, I started walking daily and cut back on refined carbs—I think it’s helping.

speaker: doctor
text: Excellent. Given your improved glucose and stable lipid profile, I’m going to hold off on intensifying diabetes prevention meds for now—but we’ll keep monitoring closely.

speaker: doctor
text: However, your hemoglobin remains at 110 g/L, and given your fatigue and hypothyroidism, I’d like to investigate iron studies and possibly add iron supplementation.

speaker: patient
text: Makes sense—I did feel a bit lightheaded when standing up quickly last week.

speaker: doctor
text: That could be orthostatic, but also consistent with mild anemia. We’ll check ferritin, TIBC, and vitamin B12 next visit.

speaker: patient
text: Right—the same number as before, 110 g/L. I remember you mentioned it last time.
